repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem Need a reliable way to tell if nvim was started from a restart ## Solution Introduce `v:startreason`
AI-assisted: Codex Closes #40184

comment_data.body:
no. there are 2 approaches i can think of:

- send a `-c ':::'` dummy arg
- use RPC
Really appreciate you taking the time to review and give suggestions. I've changed the PR to use RPC now.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -48,3 +48,11 @@ describe('v:argf', function()
     eq({ abs1, abs2, abs3 }, n.eval('v:argf'))
   end)
 end)
+
+describe('v:startreason', function()
+  it('is read-only and starts as "normal"', function()
+    n.clear()

comment_data.body: if there really is no other way internally for Nvim to (already) know it was restarted, then to signal "this is a restart" without adding another `--foo` arg, we could use a dummy `'+:::'` arg. here's an old removed example of that: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/64d55b74d83d566975e269bed0810d9008119ddf/src/nvim/ex_docmd.c#L4986-L4996
this suggests a flaw. `|v:startreason|` changes later in the startup cycle, after step 1...
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -440,18 +440,20 @@ accordingly, proceeding as follows:
 
 4. Start a server (unless |--listen| was given) and set |v:servername|.
 
-5. Wait for UI to connect.
+5. Change |v:startreason| if this is a restart.

comment_data.body:
I guess this is needed because `v:` vars aren't normally settable. And this is signaling that RPC is the wrong approach (sorry). Worse case, we could drive it via an environment variable, similar to `__NVIM_RESTART_ALLOC_CONSOLE`.

    __NVIM_RESTART
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1775,6 +1775,21 @@ void nvim__chan_set_detach(uint64_t channel_id, Boolean detach, Error *err)
   chan->detach = (bool)detach;
 }
 
+
+/// Set v:startreason variable.
+///
+/// @param reason   Startup reason ("normal", "restart").
+/// @param[out] err Error details, if any.
+void nvim__set_startreason(String reason, Error *err)

comment_data.body: Fixed in 0ca00b39f1c208b2418a61b21ecf512422e622da
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -440,18 +440,20 @@ accordingly, proceeding as follows:
 
 4. Start a server (unless |--listen| was given) and set |v:servername|.
 
-5. Wait for UI to connect.
+5. Change |v:startreason| if this is a restart.

comment_data.body: It didn't sound like there was a strong preference between this approach and the `'+:::'` dummy arg, so I went with the environment variable mainly because it was a little easier for me to tell what was going on. 0ca00b39f1c208b2418a61b21ecf512422e622da
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1775,6 +1775,21 @@ void nvim__chan_set_detach(uint64_t channel_id, Boolean detach, Error *err)
   chan->detach = (bool)detach;
 }
 
+
+/// Set v:startreason variable.
+///
+/// @param reason   Startup reason ("normal", "restart").
+/// @param[out] err Error details, if any.
+void nvim__set_startreason(String reason, Error *err)

comment_data.body: Nice work on that. I was hoping that `v:startreason` could be set at the same moment as `v:starttime` since they were related.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -440,18 +440,20 @@ accordingly, proceeding as follows:
 
 4. Start a server (unless |--listen| was given) and set |v:servername|.
 
-5. Wait for UI to connect.
+5. Change |v:startreason| if this is a restart.

comment_data.body:
Docs nit: Since `v:startreason` is set for the new Nvim server and not the current one, I would separate the mentions of `v:exitreason` and `v:startreason`. Possibly by moving the documentation of `v:startreason` to `2.`? Something like this:

```diff
                2. Starts a new Nvim server using the same |v:argv| (except
-                   `-- [file…]` files).
+                   `-- [file…]` files). |v:startreason| will be set to "restart".
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -77,7 +77,8 @@ Restart Nvim
 
                                                 *:restart*
 :restart [+cmd] [command]
-                Restarts Nvim. Sets |v:exitreason|. See also |ZR|.
+                Restarts Nvim. Sets |v:exitreason| and |v:startreason|.

comment_data.body:
there are lots of `foo_init` places, is there perhaps a better place for this rather than directly in `main` ? 

in fact `early_init` is where `v:starttime` is set...

`channel_job_start` takes an `env` dict... that would eliminate the need for `startreason_env` + `os_unsetenv(ENV_STARTREASON);`, plus it's just nicer.

(maybe `__NVIM_RESTART_ALLOC_CONSOLE` should also use that, just noticed that. but out of scope for this PR)
nice, thanks for updating the right test instead of creating a mostly-redundant new one
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -414,7 +414,15 @@ it(':restart works in headless server (no UI)', function()
     n.set_session(nil)
   end)
 
-  fn.jobstart({ n.nvim_prog, '--clean', '--headless', '--listen', server_pipe })
+  fn.jobstart({
+    n.nvim_prog,
+    '--clean',
+    '--headless',
+    '--listen',
+    server_pipe,
+    '--cmd',
+    'let g:early_startreason = v:startreason',

comment_data.body: `--cmd`
Fixed in 8b36c9dc61414910df7a45c61506aa24dcc73e7f
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -77,7 +77,8 @@ Restart Nvim
 
                                                 *:restart*
 :restart [+cmd] [command]
-                Restarts Nvim. Sets |v:exitreason|. See also |ZR|.
+                Restarts Nvim. Sets |v:exitreason| and |v:startreason|.

comment_data.body: 8b36c9dc61414910df7a45c61506aa24dcc73e7f moves this out to `evalvars_init` which is called by `early_init`
Fixed in 8b36c9dc61414910df7a45c61506aa24dcc73e7f
Should this use `create_environment` from here? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/b8e3f3f4e0c3c9b4820ec336b88dc40a62c7e724/src/nvim/eval/funcs.c#L3379-L3380

Very small nit (sorry): I do think it is important to specify what `v:startreason` is set *to*, since it is always set to something no matter what. Just adding a ` to "restart"` to this sentence I think fixes that.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -81,7 +81,8 @@ Restart Nvim
 
                 1. Stops Nvim using `:qall` (or |+cmd|, if given).
                 2. Starts a new Nvim server using the same |v:argv| (except
-                   `-- [file…]` files).
+                   `-- [file…]` files). Sets |v:startreason| on the new
+                   server.

comment_data.body: We don't specify that for `v:exitreason` just above this, but sure i can add this later today
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -81,7 +81,8 @@ Restart Nvim
 
                 1. Stops Nvim using `:qall` (or |+cmd|, if given).
                 2. Starts a new Nvim server using the same |v:argv| (except
-                   `-- [file…]` files).
+                   `-- [file…]` files). Sets |v:startreason| on the new
+                   server.

comment_data.body: That's because exitreason actually gets set at that point (it begins empty), while startreason will always have a value. I see why you thought to keep them similar though.

                 1. Stops Nvim using `:qall` (or |+cmd|, if given).
                 2. Starts a new Nvim server using the same |v:argv| (except
-                   `-- [file…]` files).
+                   `-- [file…]` files). Sets |v:startreason| on the new
+                   server.

@@ -81,7 +81,8 @@ Restart Nvim
 
                 1. Stops Nvim using `:qall` (or |+cmd|, if given).
                 2. Starts a new Nvim server using the same |v:argv| (except
-                   `-- [file…]` files).
+                   `-- [file…]` files). Sets |v:startreason| on the new

comment_data.body: hmm. i'll noodle on this in a follupw pr, let's ignore it for now.

comment_regular_data.body: Needs documentation under `:h startup` for when `v:startreason` is set (like how `v:starttime` is).

I've read through code for `ex_restart` in [ex_docmd.c](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/a69f2db773b85e03c631a5eb3a37f43098288194/src/nvim/ex_docmd.c#L4990) but I still don't understand the restart lifecycle well enough to add to `:h startup`.

Would it be between steps 4 and 5 in [`starting.txt`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/a69f2db773b85e03c631a5eb3a37f43098288194/runtime/doc/starting.txt#L443)?

> Would it be between steps 4 and 5 in [`starting.txt`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/a69f2db773b85e03c631a5eb3a37f43098288194/runtime/doc/starting.txt#L443)?

Yeah, that's probably fine for now. We may need to update those docs for "restart", haven't thought about that. I also see that `v:exitreason` is missing from that lifecycle, I'll add it.

I would need some more time to be able to prove it via the tests, but is the current approach not early enough?

Using this init.lua it is available at all of the logged times:
```lua
_G.log = {}
local log_variable = function(state) table.insert(_G.log, { state, vim.v.startreason }) end

log_variable('init.lua')
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('VimEnter', { callback = function() log_variable('VimEnter') end })
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('UIEnter', { callback = function() log_variable('UIEnter') end })
```


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9c80c35e-3a55-4d84-81c3-185e768630a1
